blocks.content: Plu, running through the alley to escape the Kergos hunters, flinched as a powerful blow struck her from the side.
As the Mecca weapon, Signa, slammed into the ground, a shockwave several meters high erupted.
Pushed by the force, Plu was thrown against the building wall along with the shockwave.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Ugh! What the…?

blocks.speaker: Raun
blocks.content: Damn it. Did I miss?
Clank. Clank.
The metallic sound pierced her eardrums like a sharp blade.
A man equipped with a Piper appeared, holding a sword and shield. It was Raun, the immortal.
At 1,010 years old, he had achieved divine transcendence at 450 and perfected his physical synchronization with machinery at 900.

blocks.speaker: Raun
blocks.content: Well, this isn't so bad. It's a woman, after all.
Raun scrutinized Plu's appearance and gave a sly smile.
Despite enjoying countless pleasures over the ages, the strongest desire for any living being remained reproduction.
For him, who had lost the concepts of love and affection, only the dry memory of pleasure remained in the back of his mind.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: What are you babbling about?
Hearing Plu's voice, Raun tilted his head and pressed a button on the Piper attached to his head.
His Mecca-level 1 equipment was perfectly customized for him, and its translation function far surpassed that of the Night Watch's devices.

blocks.speaker: Raun
blocks.content: Ah, I see. You're a feisty woman. The kind that doesn't easily fall into a man's arms.
As Raun's words were translated through the mechanical sound, Plu frowned.
Meanwhile, Raun abandoned his previously gentlemanly demeanor and revealed a face of pure, wicked desire.

blocks.speaker: Raun
blocks.content: That's my specialty.
As Plu shuddered at Raun's conveyed emotions, the ground shattered with a bang, and Raun's body shot up.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: How is that possible?
Having been part of the rebel headquarters, she had a rough understanding of the Mecca combat system.
No Piper could enhance its functions to that extent.
It wasn't a matter of performance; the human body couldn't withstand it.
Pipers were meant to assist muscle strength. If they operated faster than nerve transmission speed, just throwing a punch would dislocate a human's joints.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: But Raun's movements defied all such logic.
Perfect understanding of machinery.
In reality, Raun's Piper was 30 times more powerful than the devices used by the rebels.
It was a device that couldn't be operated without perfect synchronization of nerves and mind with the machine.
Mega Smash!
As the powerful drive of the Piper, divine transcendence, and the amplified power of Signa combined, a shockwave erupted that seemed to blow the building away.
Recognizing her opponent's capabilities, Plu drew Phoenix and fought back with all her might.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: I can't lose here…!
Plu gritted her teeth and dodged the attacks with all her strength.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: He's using some interesting techniques.
Raun quickly grasped Plu's abilities.
Based on the Nor race's abilities, but with the principles of the Mecca race.

blocks.speaker: Raun
blocks.content: As expected of hybrids, they're quite a mix.

blocks.speaker: Raun
blocks.content: This should be fun.
Raun sheathed Signa at his waist and raised his fist.
Electric Shockwave!
As he approached Plu and slammed his fist into the ground, a wave of electricity spread across a 20-meter radius.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Ugh!
Unable to handle the wide-area attack with the Dawkins Algorithm, Plu immediately disengaged the algorithm and went into defensive mode.
Raun, with explosive propulsion, lunged forward and raised his fist.
Plu crossed her arms to shield her face, layering defensive air shields to their limit.
Boom, boom, boom, boom!
Raun's fist easily pierced the heavy air barrier and struck Plu's guard.
Plu's vision blurred from the impact, and she heard the sound of her left arm breaking.
Boom!
When she regained her senses, she was rolling on the ground, not knowing how far she had been thrown.

blocks.speaker: Plu
blocks.content: Sob!
Plu let her limp left arm hang down and stood up.
Raun entered the building with a meaningful smile.

blocks.speaker: Raun
blocks.content: If I had hit your stomach, your organs would have burst, and you'd be dead.
Despite trembling, Plu glared fiercely at Raun.

blocks.speaker: Raun
blocks.content: But do you know why I hit your guard?
Raun's face lit up with the anticipated thrill of pleasure.

blocks.speaker: Raun
blocks.content: Let me teach you.
Inside Paradise, the combined combat power of the three was turning everything to ashes.
In his Vajra Armor state, Shirone was far stronger than without Arman, but the combined assault of the immortals Murka and Deina was formidable.
Murka's divine transcendence was at a level no mortal could achieve, and the magical power from Deina's mental transcendence was overwhelming, even for Shirone, who had experienced many battles.

blocks.speaker: Deina
blocks.content: Hahaha! What, was it this easy?
Deina spread wind seals in all directions, as if she had become one with the air.
Even if they could be seen, they were too fast to follow.
Murka swung his sword through the gaps in the wind seals.
With divine transcendence, there seemed to be nothing he couldn't cut, and Shirone was pushed back 100 meters in an instant.
The buildings they passed collapsed, proving the devastating power of their attacks.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This is combat based on incarnation techniques.
He had met many strong opponents, but this was his first time fighting enemies using incarnation techniques.
They manifested power through will.
It wasn't about enhancing strength but twisting the very nature of existence.
Humans, born as humans, made all decisions within the human realm.
But those who mastered incarnation techniques transcended human limits.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If humans had twelve fingers, the entire world would be different.
Similarly, those who mastered incarnation techniques had a fundamentally different sense of combat.
This created countless variables, and how they were utilized determined the vast differences in combat effectiveness.

blocks.speaker: Murka
blocks.content: Hahaha! You're completely scared. Did you think a mere mortal could defeat us? Our experience and skill are on a different level!
Murka, already feeling victorious, spread his arms wide and charged.

blocks.speaker: Murka
blocks.content: Imagining himself cutting Shirone's neck, the position of an official in the Immortal Council seemed within reach.
The Immortal Council, a group of monsters incomparable to these two, was composed of the most powerful immortals.
The ten leaders of the council had lived for at least 10,000 years, and the average age of the officials was over 3,000.

blocks.speaker: Murka
blocks.content: And now, he, a mere 1,000 years old, would become an official.
All immortals would look up to him, and he would live an even stronger and more splendid life, existing eternally in this world.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's not over yet!
Shirone glared at Murka and Deina charging from both sides.
First, he restrained Deina with Akamai, then unleashed the Photon Cannon on Murka.
The flash's power, far beyond anything before, made Murka's face pale.

blocks.speaker: Murka
blocks.content: Mental transcendence?
Even as a master of Skima, the flash's beam was so intense it left afterimages.
It was as if Shirone had caused an explosion, sending everything around him flying.
After sweeping the area with a scatter movement, he fired lasers, causing massive explosions.

blocks.speaker: Murka
blocks.content: But what truly terrified the two was not the explosions but the heat that seemed to melt their flesh.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What is this?
Shirone noticed the enemy's counterattacks weakening and observed with curious eyes.
Unlike their previous expressions of enjoyment, they now looked serious.
The biggest difference was their movements.
In a battle that should have been growing more intense, their activity had decreased.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I see.
Shirone, as if finding the solution, pressed the attack even harder.
His prediction was accurate, and Murka and Deina couldn't even think of counterattacking, only focusing on evasion.
Finally, as Shirone's Photon Cannon struck Murka's side, he flew back with a look of shock.
Boom!
Hitting the wall, he couldn't even think of checking Shirone's position, focusing instead on healing his side with Skima's regeneration.
Deina retreated towards the exit, her eyes darting as she wondered what to do.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Now I understand.
Shirone, with Akamai floating beside him, walked slowly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You are weak.
In all the heavens except for Shehakim, where Shirone had infiltrated, and Lakia, where Gaold's party was, smoke was rising.
The unified rebel headquarters was slowly advancing towards Araboth, breaking through Heaven's army, and the number of casualties increased as the distance closed.
When the giant Titans broke down Heaven's walls, the Kuroi units advanced, firing at everything in sight.
Giants beyond the seventh stage of the elixir of myths charged and toppled the Titans, while fairies destroyed the Kuroi with various spells.
The infantry suffered the most casualties.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Advance! Advance! Never retreat!
Their fury over their comrades' deaths kept their momentum from faltering.
Kanya and Lena also had no place to retreat and kept advancing.
Tens of thousands had died. The sheer number of deaths, beyond what could be calculated, drove everyone mad.
Their madness turned into fighting spirit, which they unleashed on the enemy, and the enemy's corpses further fueled the rebels' rage, making their anger even more intense.
Every time archangels or their Maras appeared, the stationed units were annihilated.
In close combat, only Babel could hold an advantage, but even she had her limits, and eventually, half of the rebel forces had perished.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Advance! Advance!
All the soldiers shouted in unison as they moved forward.
Whether they truly wanted to fight or believed they could reach their destination, such thoughts had long evaporated, leaving them to mechanically shout and advance.
It was mechanical, but in a broader sense, it was a deeply biological phenomenon.
Like ants, their individual identities had evaporated, and they were becoming a unified organism with a single purpose.

blocks.speaker: Kanya
blocks.content: Advance! Advance!
For the first time, Heaven's army felt a shiver of fear.

blocks.speaker: Heaven's leader
blocks.content: How could this be? What was driving them?
Objectively, they shouldn't have been pushed back, but the defensive line was slowly retreating.

blocks.speaker: Heaven's leader
blocks.content: They weren't fighting Lena, Kanya, or Crude.
They were fighting a perfectly unified organism, humanity itself.
